questions.solutionText: Solution We have to find the value of cos 7 π 8 + cos π 4 + cos - π 8 - 1 , using cos - θ = cos θ , = cos 7 π 8 + 1 2 + cos π 8 - 1 = cos 7 π 8 + 1 2 + cos π - 7 π 8 - 1 Using cos π - θ = - cos θ , = cos 7 π 8 + 1 2 - cos 7 π 8 - 1 = 1 2 - 1 . Now taking the options A 4 cos π 16 cos 3 π 4 cos 5 π 8 = 2 2 cos π 16 cos 5 π 8 cos π - π 4 Using 2 cos A cos B = cos A + B + cos A - B , cos π - θ = - cos θ , = 2 cos π 16 + 5 π 8 + cos π 16 - 5 π 8 - cos π 4 = 2 cos 11 π 16 + cos - 9 π 16 - 1 2 = - 2 cos 11 π 16 + cos 9 π 16 ≠ 1 2 - 1 . B 4 cos π 16 cos π 8 cos 5 π 8 = 2 2 cos π 16 cos 5 π 8 cos π 8 = 2 cos π 16 + 5 π 8 + cos π 16 - 5 π 8 cos π 8 = 2 cos 11 π 16 + cos 9 π 16 cos π 8 = 2 cos 11 π 16 cos π 8 + 2 cos 9 π 16 cos π 8 = cos 13 π 16 + cos 9 π 16 + cos 11 π 16 + cos 7 π 16 = cos 13 π 16 + cos 9 π 16 + cos 11 π 16 + cos π - 9 π 16 = cos 13 π 16 + cos 9 π 16 + cos 11 π 16 - cos 9 π 16 = cos 13 π 16 + cos 11 π 16 ≠ 1 2 - 1 . C 4 cos π 16 cos 3 π 8 cos 9 π 16 = 2 2 cos 9 π 16 cos π 16 cos 3 π 8 = 2 cos π 16 + 9 π 16 + cos 9 π 16 - π 16 cos 3 π 8 = 2 cos 10 π 16 + cos 8 π 16 cos 3 π 8 = 2 cos 5 π 8 + cos π 2 cos 3 π 8 = 2 cos 5 π 8 + 0 cos 3 π 8 = 2 cos 5 π 8 cos 3 π 8 = cos 8 π 8 + cos 2 π 8 = cos π + cos π 4 = - 1 + 1 2 . Which is same as the simplified form of the question, hence the correct option is C .
questions.solution: Solution We have to find the value of cos 7 π 8 + cos π 4 + cos - π 8 - 1 , using cos - θ = cos θ , = cos 7 π 8 + 1 2 + cos π 8 - 1 = cos 7 π 8 + 1 2 + cos π - 7 π 8 - 1 Using cos π - θ = - cos θ , = cos 7 π 8 + 1 2 - cos 7 π 8 - 1 = 1 2 - 1 . Now taking the options A 4 cos π 16 cos 3 π 4 cos 5 π 8 = 2 2 cos π 16 cos 5 π 8 cos π - π 4 Using 2 cos A cos B = cos A + B + cos A - B , cos π - θ = - cos θ , = 2 cos π 16 + 5 π 8 + cos π 16 - 5 π 8 - cos π 4 = 2 cos 11 π 16 + cos - 9 π 16 - 1 2 = - 2 cos 11 π 16 + cos 9 π 16 ≠ 1 2 - 1 . B 4 cos π 16 cos π 8 cos 5 π 8 = 2 2 cos π 16 cos 5 π 8 cos π 8 = 2 cos π 16 + 5 π 8 + cos π 16 - 5 π 8 cos π 8 = 2 cos 11 π 16 + cos 9 π 16 cos π 8 = 2 cos 11 π 16 cos π 8 + 2 cos 9 π 16 cos π 8 = cos 13 π 16 + cos 9 π 16 + cos 11 π 16 + cos 7 π 16 = cos 13 π 16 + cos 9 π 16 + cos 11 π 16 + cos π - 9 π 16 = cos 13 π 16 + cos 9 π 16 + cos 11 π 16 - cos 9 π 16 = cos 13 π 16 + cos 11 π 16 ≠ 1 2 - 1 . C 4 cos π 16 cos 3 π 8 cos 9 π 16 = 2 2 cos 9 π 16 cos π 16 cos 3 π 8 = 2 cos π 16 + 9 π 16 + cos 9 π 16 - π 16 cos 3 π 8 = 2 cos 10 π 16 + cos 8 π 16 cos 3 π 8 = 2 cos 5 π 8 + cos π 2 cos 3 π 8 = 2 cos 5 π 8 + 0 cos 3 π 8 = 2 cos 5 π 8 cos 3 π 8 = cos 8 π 8 + cos 2 π 8 = cos π + cos π 4 = - 1 + 1 2 . Which is same as the simplified form of the question, hence the correct option is C .
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

We have to find the value of cos7π8+cosπ4+cos-π8-1, using cos-θ=cosθ,

=cos7π8+12+cosπ8-1

=cos7π8+12+cosπ-7π8-1

Using cosπ-θ=-cosθ,

=cos7π8+12-cos7π8-1

=12-1.

Now taking the options

A 4cosπ16cos3π4cos5π8

=22cosπ16cos5π8cosπ-π4

Using 2cosAcosB=cosA+B+cosA-B, cosπ-θ=-cosθ,

=2cosπ16+5π8+cosπ16-5π8-cosπ4

=2cos11π16+cos-9π16-12

=-2cos11π16+cos9π16≠12-1.

B 4cosπ16cosπ8cos5π8

=22cosπ16cos5π8cosπ8

=2cosπ16+5π8+cosπ16-5π8cosπ8

=2cos11π16+cos9π16cosπ8

=2cos11π16cosπ8+2cos9π16cosπ8

=cos13π16+cos9π16+cos11π16+cos7π16

=cos13π16+cos9π16+cos11π16+cosπ-9π16

=cos13π16+cos9π16+cos11π16-cos9π16

=cos13π16+cos11π16≠12-1.

C 4cosπ16cos3π8cos9π16

=22cos9π16cosπ16cos3π8

=2cosπ16+9π16+cos9π16-π16cos3π8

=2cos10π16+cos8π16cos3π8

=2cos5π8+cosπ2cos3π8

=2cos5π8+0cos3π8

=2cos5π8cos3π8

=cos8π8+cos2π8

=cosπ+cosπ4=-1+12.

Which is same as the simplified form of the question, hence the correct option is C.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: If the equation p x 2 + 2 - q x y + 3 y 2 - 6 q x + 30 y + 6 = 0 represents a circle, then the value of p + q .
questions.questionText: If the equation p x 2 + 2 - q x y + 3 y 2 - 6 q x + 30 y + 6 = 0 represents a circle, then the value of p + q .
questions.question: If the equation p x 2 + 2 - q x y + 3 y 2 - 6 q x + 30 y + 6 = 0 represents a circle, then the value of p + q .
questions.prompt: If the equation p x 2 + 2 - q x y + 3 y 2 - 6 q x + 30 y + 6 = 0 represents a circle, then the value of p + q .
questions.html: If the equation px2+2-qxy+3y2-6qx+30y+6=0 represents a circle, then the value of p+q.
questions.questionHtml: If the equation px2+2-qxy+3y2-6qx+30y+6=0 represents a circle, then the value of p+q.
questions.solutionText: Solution The general equation of a second degree a x 2 + 2 h x y + b y 2 + 2 g x + 2 f y + c = 0 , represents a circle, if h = 0 , a = b and a g 2 + f 2 - c > 0 Hence, for the equation p x 2 + 2 - q x y + 3 y 2 - 6 q x + 30 y + 6 = 0 , we have h = 2 - q = 0 ⇒ q = 2 and a = b ⇒ p = 3 . Also, for the values of p and q the circle 3 x 2 + 3 y 2 - 12 x + 30 y + 6 = 0 ⇒ x 2 + y 2 - 4 y + 10 y + 2 = 0 g 2 + f 2 - c = - 2 2 + 5 2 - 2 = 4 + 25 - 2 = 27 > 0 For which ∴ p + q = 3 + 2 = 5
questions.solution: Solution The general equation of a second degree a x 2 + 2 h x y + b y 2 + 2 g x + 2 f y + c = 0 , represents a circle, if h = 0 , a = b and a g 2 + f 2 - c > 0 Hence, for the equation p x 2 + 2 - q x y + 3 y 2 - 6 q x + 30 y + 6 = 0 , we have h = 2 - q = 0 ⇒ q = 2 and a = b ⇒ p = 3 . Also, for the values of p and q the circle 3 x 2 + 3 y 2 - 12 x + 30 y + 6 = 0 ⇒ x 2 + y 2 - 4 y + 10 y + 2 = 0 g 2 + f 2 - c = - 2 2 + 5 2 - 2 = 4 + 25 - 2 = 27 > 0 For which ∴ p + q = 3 + 2 = 5
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

The general equation of a second degree

ax2+2hxy+by2+2gx+2fy+c=0,

represents a circle, if h=0,a=b and ag2+f2-c>0

Hence, for the equation

px2+2-qxy+3y2-6qx+30y+6=0,

we have

h=2-q=0 ⇒ q=2 and a=b ⇒ p=3.

Also, for the values of p and q the circle

3x2+3y2-12x+30y+6=0

⇒ x2+y2-4y+10y+2=0

g2+f2-c=-22+52-2=4+25-2=27>0

For which
∴ p+q=3+2=5

questions.questionHtml: In a steamer there are stalls for 15 animals and there are cows, horses and calves (not less than 15 of each) ready to be shipped. The total number of ways in which ship load can be made is
questions.solutionText: Solution Each stall can be occupied in 3 ways ; a cow or a horse or a calf may be put in it. So all the 15 stalls can be filled in 3 15 ways.

questions.questionHtml: If log23=a, log35=b, log72=c, then the logarithm of the number 63 to base 140 is
questions.solutionText: Solution Given, log 2 3 = a , log 3 5 = b , log 7 2 = c ∵ a = log 3 log 2 , b = log 5 log 3 and c = log 2 log 7 ∴ a b c = log 3 log 2 × log 5 log 3 × log 2 log 7 = log 5 log 7 2 c = 2 log 2 log 7 and a c = log 3 log 2 × log 2 log 7 = log 3 log 7 ∴ log 140 63 = log 63 log 140 = log 3 2 × 7 log 2 2 × 5 × 7 = 2 log 3 + log 7 log 7 + 2 log 2 + log 5 = 2 log 3 log 7 + 1 1 + 2 log 2 log 7 + log 5 log 7 = 2 a c + 1 1 + 2 c + a b c
questions.solution: Solution Given, log 2 3 = a , log 3 5 = b , log 7 2 = c ∵ a = log 3 log 2 , b = log 5 log 3 and c = log 2 log 7 ∴ a b c = log 3 log 2 × log 5 log 3 × log 2 log 7 = log 5 log 7 2 c = 2 log 2 log 7 and a c = log 3 log 2 × log 2 log 7 = log 3 log 7 ∴ log 140 63 = log 63 log 140 = log 3 2 × 7 log 2 2 × 5 × 7 = 2 log 3 + log 7 log 7 + 2 log 2 + log 5 = 2 log 3 log 7 + 1 1 + 2 log 2 log 7 + log 5 log 7 = 2 a c + 1 1 + 2 c + a b c
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

Given,

log23=a, log35=b, log72=c

∵ a=log3log2, b=log5log3 and c=log2log7

∴ abc=log3log2×log5log3×log2log7=log5log7

2c=2log2log7 and ac=log3log2×log2log7=log3log7

∴ log14063=log63log140=log32×7log22×5×7

=2log3+log7log7+2log2+log5

=2log3log7+11+2log2log7+log5log7

=2ac+11+2c+abc

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: Let f ( x ) satisfy the conditions of the Mean Value Theorem on the interval [ 0 , 2 ] . If f ( 0 ) = 0 and | f ′ ( x ) | ≤ 1 2 for all x in [ 0 , 2 ] , then which of the following must be true for all x in [ 0 , 2 ] ?
questions.questionText: Let f ( x ) satisfy the conditions of the Mean Value Theorem on the interval [ 0 , 2 ] . If f ( 0 ) = 0 and | f ′ ( x ) | ≤ 1 2 for all x in [ 0 , 2 ] , then which of the following must be true for all x in [ 0 , 2 ] ?
questions.question: Let f ( x ) satisfy the conditions of the Mean Value Theorem on the interval [ 0 , 2 ] . If f ( 0 ) = 0 and | f ′ ( x ) | ≤ 1 2 for all x in [ 0 , 2 ] , then which of the following must be true for all x in [ 0 , 2 ] ?
questions.prompt: Let f ( x ) satisfy the conditions of the Mean Value Theorem on the interval [ 0 , 2 ] . If f ( 0 ) = 0 and | f ′ ( x ) | ≤ 1 2 for all x in [ 0 , 2 ] , then which of the following must be true for all x in [ 0 , 2 ] ?
questions.html: Let f(x) satisfy the conditions of the Mean Value Theorem on the interval [0,2]. If f(0)=0 and |f′(x)|≤12 for all x in [0,2], then which of the following must be true for all x in [0,2]?
questions.questionHtml: Let f(x) satisfy the conditions of the Mean Value Theorem on the interval [0,2]. If f(0)=0 and |f′(x)|≤12 for all x in [0,2], then which of the following must be true for all x in [0,2]?
questions.solutionText: Solution Given: f ( 0 ) = 0 and | f ′ ( x ) | ≤ 1 2 for all x ∈ [ 0 , 2 ] . By MVT, for any x ∈ [ 0 , 2 ] , there exists c ∈ ( 0 , x ) such that: f ( x ) − f ( 0 ) = f ′ ( c ) ( x − 0 ) | f ( x ) | = | f ′ ( c ) | ⋅ | x | ≤ 1 2 ⋅ 2 = 1 So | f ( x ) | ≤ 1 for all x ∈ [ 0 , 2 ] . Checking options: (1) | f ( x ) | ≤ 1 2 : Not necessarily. Take f ( x ) = x 2 , then f ( 2 ) = 1 > 1 2 . (2) | f ( x ) | ≤ 1 : Always true as shown above. (3) f ( x ) = 2 x : Then f ′ ( x ) = 2 > 1 2 , violates the given condition. (4) f ( x ) = 3 : Since | f ( x ) | ≤ 1 , f ( x ) can never reach 3. Answer: (2) | f ( x ) | ≤ 1
questions.solution: Solution Given: f ( 0 ) = 0 and | f ′ ( x ) | ≤ 1 2 for all x ∈ [ 0 , 2 ] . By MVT, for any x ∈ [ 0 , 2 ] , there exists c ∈ ( 0 , x ) such that: f ( x ) − f ( 0 ) = f ′ ( c ) ( x − 0 ) | f ( x ) | = | f ′ ( c ) | ⋅ | x | ≤ 1 2 ⋅ 2 = 1 So | f ( x ) | ≤ 1 for all x ∈ [ 0 , 2 ] . Checking options: (1) | f ( x ) | ≤ 1 2 : Not necessarily. Take f ( x ) = x 2 , then f ( 2 ) = 1 > 1 2 . (2) | f ( x ) | ≤ 1 : Always true as shown above. (3) f ( x ) = 2 x : Then f ′ ( x ) = 2 > 1 2 , violates the given condition. (4) f ( x ) = 3 : Since | f ( x ) | ≤ 1 , f ( x ) can never reach 3. Answer: (2) | f ( x ) | ≤ 1
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Given: f(0)=0 and |f′(x)|≤12 for all x∈[0,2].

By MVT, for any x∈[0,2], there exists c∈(0,x) such that:
f(x)−f(0)=f′(c)(x−0)

|f(x)|=|f′(c)|⋅|x|≤12⋅2=1

So |f(x)|≤1 for all x∈[0,2].

Checking options:
(1) |f(x)|≤12: Not necessarily. Take f(x)=x2, then f(2)=1>12.
(2) |f(x)|≤1: Always true as shown above.
(3) f(x)=2x: Then f′(x)=2>12, violates the given condition.
(4) f(x)=3: Since |f(x)|≤1, f(x) can never reach 3.

Answer: (2) |f(x)|≤1

questions.questionHtml: Consider the family of lines x+y-1+λ2x+3y-5=0 and 3x+2y-4+μx+2y-6=0, equation of a straight line that belongs to both the families is
questions.solutionText: Solution The family of lines x + y - 1 + λ 2 x + 3 y - 5 = 0 passes through the point of intersection of x + y - 1 = 0 and 2 x + 3 y - 5 = 0 ; - 2 , 3 And family of lines 3 x + 2 y - 4 + μ x + 2 y - 6 = 0 passes through the point of intersection of 3 x + 2 y - 4 = 0 and x + 2 y - 6 = 0 ; i.e. - 1 , 7 2 ∴ Equation of the straight line that belongs to both the families passes through - 2 , 3 and - 1 , 7 2 is ⇒ y - 3 = 7 2 - 3 - 1 + 2 x + 2 ⇒ y - 3 = x + 2 2 ⇒ x - 2 y + 8 = 0
questions.solution: Solution The family of lines x + y - 1 + λ 2 x + 3 y - 5 = 0 passes through the point of intersection of x + y - 1 = 0 and 2 x + 3 y - 5 = 0 ; - 2 , 3 And family of lines 3 x + 2 y - 4 + μ x + 2 y - 6 = 0 passes through the point of intersection of 3 x + 2 y - 4 = 0 and x + 2 y - 6 = 0 ; i.e. - 1 , 7 2 ∴ Equation of the straight line that belongs to both the families passes through - 2 , 3 and - 1 , 7 2 is ⇒ y - 3 = 7 2 - 3 - 1 + 2 x + 2 ⇒ y - 3 = x + 2 2 ⇒ x - 2 y + 8 = 0
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

The family of lines x+y-1+λ2x+3y-5=0 passes through the point of intersection of x+y-1=0 and 2x+3y-5=0; -2, 3

And family of lines 3x+2y-4+μx+2y-6=0 passes through the point of intersection of 3x+2y-4=0 and x+2y-6=0; i.e. -1, 72

∴ Equation of the straight line that belongs to both the families passes through -2, 3 and -1, 72 is
⇒ y-3=72-3-1+2x+2
⇒y-3=x+22

⇒x-2y+8=0

questions.text: The name of the curve described parametrically by the equations x = t 2 + t + 1 , y = t 2 - t + 1 , is
questions.questionText: The name of the curve described parametrically by the equations x = t 2 + t + 1 , y = t 2 - t + 1 , is
questions.question: The name of the curve described parametrically by the equations x = t 2 + t + 1 , y = t 2 - t + 1 , is
questions.prompt: The name of the curve described parametrically by the equations x = t 2 + t + 1 , y = t 2 - t + 1 , is
questions.html: The name of the curve described parametrically by the equations x=t2+t+1, y=t2-t+1, is
questions.questionHtml: The name of the curve described parametrically by the equations x=t2+t+1, y=t2-t+1, is
questions.solutionText: Solution Here, we are having x = t 2 + t + 1 , y = t 2 - t + 1 , On subtracting y from x , we get x - y = t 2 + t + 1 - t 2 - t + 1 ⇒ x - y = 2 t or t = x - y 2 , On putting the value of t , in x , we get x = x - y 2 2 + x - y 2 + 1 , On further simplification, we get x = x 2 - 2 x y + y 2 4 + x - y 2 + 1 , ⇒ 4 x = x 2 - 2 x y + y 2 + 2 x - 2 y + 4 , Final equation, we get as x 2 - 2 x y + y 2 - 2 x - 2 y + 4 = 0 , On comparing, this with the general equation of second degree, i.e., a x 2 + 2 h x y + b y 2 + 2 g x + 2 f y + c = 0 , we will get a = 1 , h = - 1 , b = 1 , g = - 1 , f = - 1 , c = 4 . Now, to check for the type of conic, we will check ∆ = a b c + 2 f g h - a f 2 - b g 2 - c h 2 and h 2 - a b . ∆ = 4 - 2 - 1 - 1 - 4 = - 4 ≠ 0 , Now, h 2 - a b = 1 - 1 = 0 . As, we are having ∆ ≠ 0 and h 2 - a b = 0 , which is the condition for a parabola.
questions.solution: Solution Here, we are having x = t 2 + t + 1 , y = t 2 - t + 1 , On subtracting y from x , we get x - y = t 2 + t + 1 - t 2 - t + 1 ⇒ x - y = 2 t or t = x - y 2 , On putting the value of t , in x , we get x = x - y 2 2 + x - y 2 + 1 , On further simplification, we get x = x 2 - 2 x y + y 2 4 + x - y 2 + 1 , ⇒ 4 x = x 2 - 2 x y + y 2 + 2 x - 2 y + 4 , Final equation, we get as x 2 - 2 x y + y 2 - 2 x - 2 y + 4 = 0 , On comparing, this with the general equation of second degree, i.e., a x 2 + 2 h x y + b y 2 + 2 g x + 2 f y + c = 0 , we will get a = 1 , h = - 1 , b = 1 , g = - 1 , f = - 1 , c = 4 . Now, to check for the type of conic, we will check ∆ = a b c + 2 f g h - a f 2 - b g 2 - c h 2 and h 2 - a b . ∆ = 4 - 2 - 1 - 1 - 4 = - 4 ≠ 0 , Now, h 2 - a b = 1 - 1 = 0 . As, we are having ∆ ≠ 0 and h 2 - a b = 0 , which is the condition for a parabola.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

Here, we are having x=t2+t+1, y=t2-t+1,

On subtracting y from x, we get

x-y=t2+t+1-t2-t+1

⇒x-y=2t or t=x - y2,

On putting the value of t, in x, we get

x = x - y22 + x - y2 + 1,

On further simplification, we get

x=x2 - 2xy + y24+x - y2+1,

⇒4x=x2-2xy+y2+2x-2y+4,

Final equation, we get as x2-2xy+y2-2x-2y+4=0,

On comparing, this with the general equation of second degree, i.e., ax2+2hxy+by2+2gx+2fy+c=0,

we will get a=1, h=-1, b=1, g=-1, f=-1, c=4.

Now, to check for the type of conic, we will check

∆ = abc + 2fgh - af2 - bg2 - ch2 and h2 - ab.

∆=4 -2-1-1-4=-4≠0,

Now, h2-ab=1-1=0.

As, we are having ∆ ≠ 0 and h2-ab=0, which is the condition for a parabola.

questions.question: The equation of the line passing through ( 1 , 2 , 3 ) and parallel to the planes r ⋅ ( i ^ − j ^ + 2 k ^ ) = 5 and r. ( 3 i ^ + j ^ + k ^ ) = 6 is
questions.prompt: The equation of the line passing through ( 1 , 2 , 3 ) and parallel to the planes r ⋅ ( i ^ − j ^ + 2 k ^ ) = 5 and r. ( 3 i ^ + j ^ + k ^ ) = 6 is

questions.questionHtml: The equation of the line passing through (1,2,3) and parallel to the planes r⋅(i^−j^+2k^)=5 and r. (3i^+j^+k^)=6 is
questions.solutionText: Solution The equation of line passing through ( 1 , 2 , 3) and parallel to b is given by r = ( i ^ + 2 j ^ + 3 k ^ ) + λ ( b 1 i ^ + b 2 j ^ + b 3 k ^ ) ...(i) The equations of the given planes are r ⋅ ( i ^ − j ^ + 2 k ^ ) = 5 . . . ( i i ) and r ⋅ ( 3 i ^ + j ^ + k ^ ) = 6 . . . ( i i i ) The line in eq. (i) and plane in eq. (ii) are parallel. Therefore, the normal to the plane of eq. (ii) and the given line are perpendicular. ∴ ( i ^ − j ^ + 2 k ^ ) ⋅ λ ( b 1 i ^ + b 2 j ^ + b 3 k ^ ) = 0 ⇒ λ ( b 1 − b 2 + 2 b 3 ) = 0 ⇒ ( b 1 − b 2 + 2 b 3 ) = 0 . . . ( i v ) Similarly, ( 3 i ^ + j ^ + k ^ ) ⋅ λ ( b 1 i ^ + b 2 j ^ + b 3 k ^ ) = 0 ⇒ λ ( 3 b 1 + b 2 + b 3 ) = 0 . . . ( v ) From eqs. (iv) and (v), we obtain b 1 ( − 1 ) × 1 − 1 × 2 = b 2 2 × 3 − 1 × 1 = b 3 1 × 1 − 3 ( − 1 ) ⇒ b 1 − 3 = b 2 5 = b 3 4 Therefore, the direction ratios of b are − 3 , 5 and 4 . ∴ b = b 1 i ^ + b 2 j ^ + b 3 k ^ = − 3 i ^ + 5 j ^ + 4 k ^ Substituting the value of b in eq. (i), we obtain r = ( i ^ + 2 j ^ + 3 k ^ ) + λ ( − 3 i ^ + 5 j ^ + 4 k ^ ) This is the equation of the required line.
questions.solution: Solution The equation of line passing through ( 1 , 2 , 3) and parallel to b is given by r = ( i ^ + 2 j ^ + 3 k ^ ) + λ ( b 1 i ^ + b 2 j ^ + b 3 k ^ ) ...(i) The equations of the given planes are r ⋅ ( i ^ − j ^ + 2 k ^ ) = 5 . . . ( i i ) and r ⋅ ( 3 i ^ + j ^ + k ^ ) = 6 . . . ( i i i ) The line in eq. (i) and plane in eq. (ii) are parallel. Therefore, the normal to the plane of eq. (ii) and the given line are perpendicular. ∴ ( i ^ − j ^ + 2 k ^ ) ⋅ λ ( b 1 i ^ + b 2 j ^ + b 3 k ^ ) = 0 ⇒ λ ( b 1 − b 2 + 2 b 3 ) = 0 ⇒ ( b 1 − b 2 + 2 b 3 ) = 0 . . . ( i v ) Similarly, ( 3 i ^ + j ^ + k ^ ) ⋅ λ ( b 1 i ^ + b 2 j ^ + b 3 k ^ ) = 0 ⇒ λ ( 3 b 1 + b 2 + b 3 ) = 0 . . . ( v ) From eqs. (iv) and (v), we obtain b 1 ( − 1 ) × 1 − 1 × 2 = b 2 2 × 3 − 1 × 1 = b 3 1 × 1 − 3 ( − 1 ) ⇒ b 1 − 3 = b 2 5 = b 3 4 Therefore, the direction ratios of b are − 3 , 5 and 4 . ∴ b = b 1 i ^ + b 2 j ^ + b 3 k ^ = − 3 i ^ + 5 j ^ + 4 k ^ Substituting the value of b in eq. (i), we obtain r = ( i ^ + 2 j ^ + 3 k ^ ) + λ ( − 3 i ^ + 5 j ^ + 4 k ^ ) This is the equation of the required line.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
The equation of line passing through (1,2,
3) and parallel to b is given by
r=(i^+2j^+3k^)+λ(b1i^+b2j^+b3k^) ...(i)
The equations of the given planes are
r⋅(i^−j^+2k^)=5...(ii) and r⋅(3i^+j^+k^)=6...(iii)
The line in eq. (i) and plane in eq. (ii) are parallel. Therefore, the normal to the plane of eq. (ii) and the given line are perpendicular.
∴(i^−j^+2k^)⋅λ(b1i^+b2j^+b3k^)=0⇒λ(b1−b2+2b3)=0⇒(b1−b2+2b3)=0...(iv)
Similarly,
(3i^+j^+k^)⋅λ(b1i^+b2j^+b3k^)=0⇒λ(3b1+b2+b3)=0...(v)
From eqs. (iv) and (v), we obtain
b1(−1)×1−1×2=b22×3−1×1=b31×1−3(−1)⇒b1−3=b25=b34
Therefore, the direction ratios of b are −3,5 and 4 .
∴b=b1i^+b2j^+b3k^=−3i^+5j^+4k^
Substituting the value of b in eq. (i), we obtain
r=(i^+2j^+3k^)+λ(−3i^+5j^+4k^)
This is the equation of the required line.

( i i ) Therefore, equations of bisectors of these lines are m x 2 + 2 x y − m y 2 = 0. . . ( i i i ) n x 2 + 2 x y − n y 2 = 0. . . ( i v ) But according to the condition (i) and (iv), (ii) and (iii) must be coincident, ∴ n 1 = 2 − 2 m = − n − 1 ⇒ m n = − 1 .

questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Here equations of pair of straight lines are
x2−2mxy−y2=0...(i)x2−2nxy−y2=0...(ii)
Therefore, equations of bisectors of these lines are
mx2+2xy−my2=0...(iii)nx2+2xy−ny2=0...(iv)
But according to the condition (i) and (iv), (ii) and (iii) must be coincident,
∴ n1=2−2m=−n−1⇒mn=−1.

questions.text: The number of common tangents to the circles x 2 + y 2 = 4 and x 2 + y 2 - 6 x - 8 y = 24 is
questions.questionText: The number of common tangents to the circles x 2 + y 2 = 4 and x 2 + y 2 - 6 x - 8 y = 24 is
questions.question: The number of common tangents to the circles x 2 + y 2 = 4 and x 2 + y 2 - 6 x - 8 y = 24 is
questions.prompt: The number of common tangents to the circles x 2 + y 2 = 4 and x 2 + y 2 - 6 x - 8 y = 24 is
questions.html: The number of common tangents to the circles x2+y2=4 and x2+y2-6x-8y=24 is
questions.questionHtml: The number of common tangents to the circles x2+y2=4 and x2+y2-6x-8y=24 is
questions.solutionText: Solution For the circle x 2 + y 2 = 4 centre is C 1 0 , 0 and the radius r 1 = 2 . For the circle x 2 + y 2 - 6 x - 8 y = 24 centre is C 2 ( 3 , 4 ) and radius is r 2 = 3 2 + 4 2 - - 24 = 7 Therefore, r 1 + r 2 = 2 + 7 = 9 . C 1 C 2 = 3 - 0 2 + 4 - 0 2 = 5 ⇒ C 1 C 2 < r 1 + r 2 r 2 - r 1 = 7 - 2 = 5 ⇒ C 1 C 2 = r 2 - r 1 Thus, the given circles touch each other internally. Hence, the number of common tangents is only one.
questions.solution: Solution For the circle x 2 + y 2 = 4 centre is C 1 0 , 0 and the radius r 1 = 2 . For the circle x 2 + y 2 - 6 x - 8 y = 24 centre is C 2 ( 3 , 4 ) and radius is r 2 = 3 2 + 4 2 - - 24 = 7 Therefore, r 1 + r 2 = 2 + 7 = 9 . C 1 C 2 = 3 - 0 2 + 4 - 0 2 = 5 ⇒ C 1 C 2 < r 1 + r 2 r 2 - r 1 = 7 - 2 = 5 ⇒ C 1 C 2 = r 2 - r 1 Thus, the given circles touch each other internally. Hence, the number of common tangents is only one.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

For the circle x2+y2=4 centre is C10,0 and the radius r1=2.

For the circle x2+y2-6x-8y=24 centre is C2(3,4) and radius is r2=32+42--24= 7

Therefore, r1+r2=2+7=9.

C1C2=3-02+4-02 =5⇒C1C2<r1+r2

r2-r1=7-2=5⇒C1C2=r2-r1

Thus, the given circles touch each other internally. Hence, the number of common tangents is only one.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: A factory makes tennis rackets and cricket bats. A tennis racket takes 1.5 hours of machine time and 3 hours of craftsman's time in its making while a cricket bat takes 3 hour of machine time and 1 hour of craftsman's time. In a day, the factory has the availability of not more than 42 hours of machine time and 24 hours of craftsman's time. If the profit on a racket and on a bat is Rs. 20 and Rs. 10 respectively, find the maximum profit of the factory when it works at full capacity.

questions.questionHtml: A factory makes tennis rackets and cricket bats. A tennis racket takes 1.5 hours of machine time and 3 hours of craftsman's time in its making while a cricket bat takes 3 hour of machine time and 1 hour of craftsman's time. In a day, the factory has the availability of not more than 42 hours of machine time and 24 hours of craftsman's time. If the profit on a racket and on a bat is Rs. 20 and Rs. 10 respectively, find the maximum profit of the factory when it works at full capacity.
questions.solutionText: Solution Let x = number of tennis rackets, y = number of cricket bats. Constraints: Machine time: 1.5 x + 3 y ≤ 42 ⇒ x + 2 y ≤ 28 ⋯ ( 1 ) Craftsman's time: 3 x + y ≤ 24 ⋯ ( 2 ) x ≥ 0 , y ≥ 0 Maximize: P = 20 x + 10 y Corner points: ( 0 , 0 ) : P = 0 ( 8 , 0 ) : from (2), 3 x = 24 ⇒ x = 8 . Check (1): 8 ≤ 28 . P = 160 ( 0 , 14 ) : from (1), 2 y = 28 ⇒ y = 14 . Check (2): 14 ≤ 24 . P = 140 Intersection of (1) and (2): x + 2 y = 28 and 3 x + y = 24 From (1): x = 28 − 2 y 3 ( 28 − 2 y ) + y = 24 84 − 6 y + y = 24 − 5 y = − 60 ⇒ y = 12 x = 28 − 24 = 4 ( 4 , 12 ) : Check constraints: 1.5 ( 4 ) + 3 ( 12 ) = 42 and 3 ( 4 ) + 12 = 24 . Both satisfied. P = 20 ( 4 ) + 10 ( 12 ) = 80 + 120 = 200 Maximum profit = Rs. 200 at x = 4 , y = 12 . Answer: (1) maximum profit is Rs. 200, when 4 tennis rackets and 12 cricket bats are produced.
questions.solution: Solution Let x = number of tennis rackets, y = number of cricket bats. Constraints: Machine time: 1.5 x + 3 y ≤ 42 ⇒ x + 2 y ≤ 28 ⋯ ( 1 ) Craftsman's time: 3 x + y ≤ 24 ⋯ ( 2 ) x ≥ 0 , y ≥ 0 Maximize: P = 20 x + 10 y Corner points: ( 0 , 0 ) : P = 0 ( 8 , 0 ) : from (2), 3 x = 24 ⇒ x = 8 . Check (1): 8 ≤ 28 . P = 160 ( 0 , 14 ) : from (1), 2 y = 28 ⇒ y = 14 . Check (2): 14 ≤ 24 . P = 140 Intersection of (1) and (2): x + 2 y = 28 and 3 x + y = 24 From (1): x = 28 − 2 y 3 ( 28 − 2 y ) + y = 24 84 − 6 y + y = 24 − 5 y = − 60 ⇒ y = 12 x = 28 − 24 = 4 ( 4 , 12 ) : Check constraints: 1.5 ( 4 ) + 3 ( 12 ) = 42 and 3 ( 4 ) + 12 = 24 . Both satisfied. P = 20 ( 4 ) + 10 ( 12 ) = 80 + 120 = 200 Maximum profit = Rs. 200 at x = 4 , y = 12 . Answer: (1) maximum profit is Rs. 200, when 4 tennis rackets and 12 cricket bats are produced.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Let x = number of tennis rackets, y = number of cricket bats.

Constraints:
Machine time: 1.5x+3y≤42⇒x+2y≤28 ⋯(1)
Craftsman's time: 3x+y≤24 ⋯(2)
x≥0,y≥0

Maximize: P=20x+10y

Corner points:
(0,0): P=0
(8,0): from (2), 3x=24⇒x=8. Check (1): 8≤28. P=160
(0,14): from (1), 2y=28⇒y=14. Check (2): 14≤24. P=140

Intersection of (1) and (2):
x+2y=28 and 3x+y=24
From (1): x=28−2y
3(28−2y)+y=24
84−6y+y=24
−5y=−60⇒y=12
x=28−24=4

(4,12): Check constraints: 1.5(4)+3(12)=42 and 3(4)+12=24. Both satisfied.
P=20(4)+10(12)=80+120=200

Maximum profit = Rs. 200 at x=4,y=12.

Answer: (1) maximum profit is Rs. 200, when 4 tennis rackets and 12 cricket bats are produced.

questions.solutionText: Solution a a + x 1 2 + a a - x 1 2 = 1 1 + x a 1 2 + 1 1 - x a 1 2 = 1 + x a - 1 2 + 1 + - x a - 1 2 = 1 + - 1 2 x a + - 1 2 - 3 2 1.2 x a 2 + 1 + - 1 2 - x a + - 1 2 - 3 2 1.2 - x a 2 (Neglecting x a 3 and higher powers) = 1 - x 2 a + 3 x 2 8 a 2 + 1 + x 2 a + 3 x 2 8 a 2 = 2 + 3 x 2 4 a 2 ∴ k = 3 4 .

questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

aa+x12+aa-x12

=11+xa12+ 11-xa12

=1+xa-12+1+-xa-12

=1+-12 xa+-12 -321.2 xa2+1+-12 -xa+-12 -321.2 -xa2

(Neglecting xa3 and higher powers)

=1-x2a+3x28a2+1+x2a+3x28a2=2+3x24a2

∴ k=34.

questions.html: Let f:R-α6→R be defined by fx=5x+36x-α. Then the value of α for which fofx=x, for all x∈R-α6, is
questions.questionHtml: Let f:R-α6→R be defined by fx=5x+36x-α. Then the value of α for which fofx=x, for all x∈R-α6, is
questions.solutionText: Solution We have, f x = y = 5 x + 3 6 x - α … i ⇒ 5 x + 3 = 6 x y - α y ⇒ x ( 6 y - 5 ) = α y + 3 ⇒ x = α y + 3 6 y - 5 Hence, f - 1 x = α x + 3 6 x - 5 … ii Now, f o f x = x f ( x ) = f - 1 ( x ) From equation i and i i , we have 5 x + 3 6 x - α = α x + 3 6 x - 5 Clearly, α = 5
questions.solution: Solution We have, f x = y = 5 x + 3 6 x - α … i ⇒ 5 x + 3 = 6 x y - α y ⇒ x ( 6 y - 5 ) = α y + 3 ⇒ x = α y + 3 6 y - 5 Hence, f - 1 x = α x + 3 6 x - 5 … ii Now, f o f x = x f ( x ) = f - 1 ( x ) From equation i and i i , we have 5 x + 3 6 x - α = α x + 3 6 x - 5 Clearly, α = 5
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

We have,

fx=y=5x+36x-α …i

⇒5x+3=6xy-αy

⇒x(6y-5)=αy+3

⇒x=αy+36y-5

Hence,

f-1x=αx+36x-5 …ii

Now,

fofx=x

f(x)=f-1(x)

From equation i and ii, we have

5x+36x-α=αx+36x-5

Clearly, α=5

questions.prompt: If the circle x 2 + y 2 + 2 g x + 2 f y + c = 0 cuts the three circles x 2 + y 2 − 5 = 0 , x 2 + y 2 − 8 x − 6 y + 10 = 0 , and x 2 + y 2 − 4 x + 2 y − 2 = 0 at the extremities of their diameters, then which of the following is true?
questions.html: If the circle x2+y2+2gx+2fy+c=0 cuts the three circles x2+y2−5=0, x2+y2−8x−6y+10=0, and x2+y2−4x+2y−2=0 at the extremities of their diameters, then which of the following is true?
questions.questionHtml: If the circle x2+y2+2gx+2fy+c=0 cuts the three circles x2+y2−5=0, x2+y2−8x−6y+10=0, and x2+y2−4x+2y−2=0 at the extremities of their diameters, then which of the following is true?
questions.solutionText: Solution If a circle cuts another circle at the extremities of a diameter, then the common chord is a diameter of the second circle, meaning the common chord passes through the centre of the second circle. Common chord of circle S and S i is given by S − S i = 0 . Circle 1: x 2 + y 2 − 5 = 0 , centre ( 0 , 0 ) Common chord: 2 g x + 2 f y + c + 5 = 0 Passes through ( 0 , 0 ) : c + 5 = 0 ⇒ c = − 5 ⋯ ( 1 ) Circle 2: x 2 + y 2 − 8 x − 6 y + 10 = 0 , centre ( 4 , 3 ) Common chord: ( 2 g + 8 ) x + ( 2 f + 6 ) y + c − 10 = 0 Passes through ( 4 , 3 ) : 4 ( 2 g + 8 ) + 3 ( 2 f + 6 ) + c − 10 = 0 8 g + 32 + 6 f + 18 + c − 10 = 0 8 g + 6 f + c + 40 = 0 8 g + 6 f − 5 + 40 = 0 8 g + 6 f + 35 = 0 ⋯ ( 2 ) Circle 3: x 2 + y 2 − 4 x + 2 y − 2 = 0 , centre ( 2 , − 1 ) Common chord: ( 2 g + 4 ) x + ( 2 f − 2 ) y + c + 2 = 0 Passes through ( 2 , − 1 ) : 2 ( 2 g + 4 ) + ( − 1 ) ( 2 f − 2 ) + c + 2 = 0 4 g + 8 − 2 f + 2 + c + 2 = 0 4 g − 2 f + c + 12 = 0 4 g − 2 f − 5 + 12 = 0 4 g − 2 f + 7 = 0 ⋯ ( 3 ) From (2): 8 g + 6 f = − 35 From (3): 4 g − 2 f = − 7 ⇒ 12 g − 6 f = − 21 Adding (2) and this: 20 g = − 56 ⇒ g = − 56 20 = − 14 5 From (3): 4 ( − 14 5 ) − 2 f = − 7 − 56 5 − 2 f = − 7 − 2 f = − 7 + 56 5 = 21 5 f = − 21 10 (1) c = − 5 ≠ 5 (2) f g = ( − 21 10 ) ( − 14 5 ) = 294 50 = 147 25 (Correct) (3) g + 2 f = − 14 5 − 42 10 = − 14 5 − 21 5 = − 7 , c + 2 = − 3 ⇒ not equal (4) 3 f = − 63 10 , 4 g = − 56 5 = − 112 10 ⇒ not equal
questions.solution: Solution If a circle cuts another circle at the extremities of a diameter, then the common chord is a diameter of the second circle, meaning the common chord passes through the centre of the second circle. Common chord of circle S and S i is given by S − S i = 0 . Circle 1: x 2 + y 2 − 5 = 0 , centre ( 0 , 0 ) Common chord: 2 g x + 2 f y + c + 5 = 0 Passes through ( 0 , 0 ) : c + 5 = 0 ⇒ c = − 5 ⋯ ( 1 ) Circle 2: x 2 + y 2 − 8 x − 6 y + 10 = 0 , centre ( 4 , 3 ) Common chord: ( 2 g + 8 ) x + ( 2 f + 6 ) y + c − 10 = 0 Passes through ( 4 , 3 ) : 4 ( 2 g + 8 ) + 3 ( 2 f + 6 ) + c − 10 = 0 8 g + 32 + 6 f + 18 + c − 10 = 0 8 g + 6 f + c + 40 = 0 8 g + 6 f − 5 + 40 = 0 8 g + 6 f + 35 = 0 ⋯ ( 2 ) Circle 3: x 2 + y 2 − 4 x + 2 y − 2 = 0 , centre ( 2 , − 1 ) Common chord: ( 2 g + 4 ) x + ( 2 f − 2 ) y + c + 2 = 0 Passes through ( 2 , − 1 ) : 2 ( 2 g + 4 ) + ( − 1 ) ( 2 f − 2 ) + c + 2 = 0 4 g + 8 − 2 f + 2 + c + 2 = 0 4 g − 2 f + c + 12 = 0 4 g − 2 f − 5 + 12 = 0 4 g − 2 f + 7 = 0 ⋯ ( 3 ) From (2): 8 g + 6 f = − 35 From (3): 4 g − 2 f = − 7 ⇒ 12 g − 6 f = − 21 Adding (2) and this: 20 g = − 56 ⇒ g = − 56 20 = − 14 5 From (3): 4 ( − 14 5 ) − 2 f = − 7 − 56 5 − 2 f = − 7 − 2 f = − 7 + 56 5 = 21 5 f = − 21 10 (1) c = − 5 ≠ 5 (2) f g = ( − 21 10 ) ( − 14 5 ) = 294 50 = 147 25 (Correct) (3) g + 2 f = − 14 5 − 42 10 = − 14 5 − 21 5 = − 7 , c + 2 = − 3 ⇒ not equal (4) 3 f = − 63 10 , 4 g = − 56 5 = − 112 10 ⇒ not equal
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
If a circle cuts another circle at the extremities of a diameter, then the common chord is a diameter of the second circle, meaning the common chord passes through the centre of the second circle.

Common chord of circle S and Si is given by S−Si=0.

Circle 1: x2+y2−5=0, centre (0,0)
Common chord: 2gx+2fy+c+5=0
Passes through (0,0): c+5=0⇒c=−5 ⋯(1)

Circle 2: x2+y2−8x−6y+10=0, centre (4,3)
Common chord: (2g+8)x+(2f+6)y+c−10=0
Passes through (4,3): 4(2g+8)+3(2f+6)+c−10=0
8g+32+6f+18+c−10=0
8g+6f+c+40=0
8g+6f−5+40=0
8g+6f+35=0 ⋯(2)

Passes through (2,−1): 2(2g+4)+(−1)(2f−2)+c+2=0

4g−2f+c+12=0
4g−2f−5+12=0
4g−2f+7=0 ⋯(3)

From (2): 8g+6f=−35
From (3): 4g−2f=−7⇒12g−6f=−21

Adding (2) and this: 20g=−56⇒g=−5620=−145

From (3): 4(−145)−2f=−7
−565−2f=−7
−2f=−7+565=215
f=−2110

(1) c=−5≠5
(2) fg=(−2110)(−145)=29450=14725 (Correct)
(3) g+2f=−145−4210=−145−215=−7, c+2=−3 ⇒ not equal
(4) 3f=−6310, 4g=−565=−11210 ⇒ not equal

questions.questionHtml: If a∈R and the equation −3(x−[x])2+2(x−[x])+a2=0 (where [x] denotes the greatest integer ≤x) has at least one real solution, but no integral solution, then all possible values of a lie in the interval:
questions.solutionText: Solution Let { x } = x − [ x ] (fractional part), so { x } ∈ [ 0 , 1 ) . The equation becomes: − 3 { x } 2 + 2 { x } + a 2 = 0 a 2 = 3 { x } 2 − 2 { x } Let t = { x } ∈ [ 0 , 1 ) a 2 = 3 t 2 − 2 t = t ( 3 t − 2 ) Let h ( t ) = 3 t 2 − 2 t for t ∈ [ 0 , 1 ) h ′ ( t ) = 6 t − 2 = 0 ⇒ t = 1 3 h ( 0 ) = 0 , h ( 1 3 ) = 1 3 − 2 3 = − 1 3 , h ( 1 − ) = 3 − 2 = 1 Range of h ( t ) for t ∈ [ 0 , 1 ) is [ − 1 3 , 1 ) So a 2 ∈ [ − 1 3 , 1 ) , and since a 2 ≥ 0 : a 2 ∈ [ 0 , 1 ) No integral solution means t ≠ 0 (since { x } = 0 when x is an integer). At t = 0 : a 2 = 0 ⇒ a = 0 So we must exclude a = 0 , giving a 2 ∈ ( 0 , 1 ) | a | ∈ ( 0 , 1 ) a ∈ ( − 1 , 0 ) ∪ ( 0 , 1 )
questions.solution: Solution Let { x } = x − [ x ] (fractional part), so { x } ∈ [ 0 , 1 ) . The equation becomes: − 3 { x } 2 + 2 { x } + a 2 = 0 a 2 = 3 { x } 2 − 2 { x } Let t = { x } ∈ [ 0 , 1 ) a 2 = 3 t 2 − 2 t = t ( 3 t − 2 ) Let h ( t ) = 3 t 2 − 2 t for t ∈ [ 0 , 1 ) h ′ ( t ) = 6 t − 2 = 0 ⇒ t = 1 3 h ( 0 ) = 0 , h ( 1 3 ) = 1 3 − 2 3 = − 1 3 , h ( 1 − ) = 3 − 2 = 1 Range of h ( t ) for t ∈ [ 0 , 1 ) is [ − 1 3 , 1 ) So a 2 ∈ [ − 1 3 , 1 ) , and since a 2 ≥ 0 : a 2 ∈ [ 0 , 1 ) No integral solution means t ≠ 0 (since { x } = 0 when x is an integer). At t = 0 : a 2 = 0 ⇒ a = 0 So we must exclude a = 0 , giving a 2 ∈ ( 0 , 1 ) | a | ∈ ( 0 , 1 ) a ∈ ( − 1 , 0 ) ∪ ( 0 , 1 )
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Let {x}=x−[x] (fractional part), so {x}∈[0,1).

The equation becomes: −3{x}2+2{x}+a2=0

a2=3{x}2−2{x}

Let t={x}∈[0,1)

a2=3t2−2t=t(3t−2)

Let h(t)=3t2−2t for t∈[0,1)

h′(t)=6t−2=0⇒t=13

h(0)=0, h(13)=13−23=−13, h(1−)=3−2=1

Range of h(t) for t∈[0,1) is [−13,1)

So a2∈[−13,1), and since a2≥0: a2∈[0,1)

No integral solution means t≠0 (since {x}=0 when x is an integer).

At t=0: a2=0⇒a=0

So we must exclude a=0, giving a2∈(0,1)

|a|∈(0,1)

a∈(−1,0)∪(0,1)

questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
log2⁡(9x−1+7)−log2⁡(3x−1+1)=2
⇒log2⁡(9x−1+73x−1+1)=2log2⁡2
⇒log2⁡(9x−1+73x−1+1)=log2⁡22
⇒(9x−1+73x−1+1)=4
⇒(32)x−1+7(3x−1+1)=4
⇒(3x−1)2+73x−1+1=4
Let 3x−1=y
∴y2+7y+1=4
⇒y2+7=4y+4
⇒y2−4y+3=0
⇒y2−3y−y+3=0
⇒y(y−3)−1(y−3)=0
⇒(y−3)(y−1)=0
⇒y=3,1
If y=3, then
3x−1=3
⇒x−1=1⇒x=2
If y=1, then
3x−1=30
⇒x−1=0⇒x=1
∴x=1,2

questions.solutionText: Solution The equation of the given ellipse is x 2 9 + y 2 1 6 = 1 The equation of the director circle is x 2 + y 2 = 9 + 16 ⇒ x 2 + y 2 = 25 On substituting the given point in the above equation, we get 9 + 16 = 25 ∴ LHS = RHS The given point lies on the director circle of the given ellipse. The angle between the tangents drawn from the given point to the ellipse will be 90 o . ∴ α = 90 ∘ ∴ sin θ + cos ( 90 ∘ + θ ) = sin θ + ( - sin θ ) = 0 Hence, sin θ + cos ( 90 ∘ + θ ) = 0 Alternatively Joint equation of PR and QR using the point (3, 4) and the ellipse x 2 9 + y 2 1 6 = 1 is S S 1 = T 2 (also called as S 1 2 = S × S 1 1 ) ⇒ 9 6 + 1 6 1 6 - 1 x 2 9 + y 2 1 6 - 1 = 3 x 9 + 4 y 1 6 - 1 2 ⇒ x 2 9 + y 2 1 6 - 1 = x 3 + y 4 - 1 2 ⇒ x 2 9 + y 2 1 6 - 1 = x 2 9 + y 2 1 6 + xy 6 - y 2 - 2 x 3 ⇒ xy 6 - y 2 - 2 x 3 + 1 = 0 ∴ Coefficient of x 2 + Coefficient of y 2 = 0 The lines PQ and PR are perpendicular. The required angle between the tangents is 90 o . ∴ α = 90 ∘ ∴ sin θ + cos ( 90 ∘ + θ ) = sin θ + ( - sin θ ) = 0 Hence, sin θ + cos ( 90 ∘ + θ ) = 0
questions.solution: Solution The equation of the given ellipse is x 2 9 + y 2 1 6 = 1 The equation of the director circle is x 2 + y 2 = 9 + 16 ⇒ x 2 + y 2 = 25 On substituting the given point in the above equation, we get 9 + 16 = 25 ∴ LHS = RHS The given point lies on the director circle of the given ellipse. The angle between the tangents drawn from the given point to the ellipse will be 90 o . ∴ α = 90 ∘ ∴ sin θ + cos ( 90 ∘ + θ ) = sin θ + ( - sin θ ) = 0 Hence, sin θ + cos ( 90 ∘ + θ ) = 0 Alternatively Joint equation of PR and QR using the point (3, 4) and the ellipse x 2 9 + y 2 1 6 = 1 is S S 1 = T 2 (also called as S 1 2 = S × S 1 1 ) ⇒ 9 6 + 1 6 1 6 - 1 x 2 9 + y 2 1 6 - 1 = 3 x 9 + 4 y 1 6 - 1 2 ⇒ x 2 9 + y 2 1 6 - 1 = x 3 + y 4 - 1 2 ⇒ x 2 9 + y 2 1 6 - 1 = x 2 9 + y 2 1 6 + xy 6 - y 2 - 2 x 3 ⇒ xy 6 - y 2 - 2 x 3 + 1 = 0 ∴ Coefficient of x 2 + Coefficient of y 2 = 0 The lines PQ and PR are perpendicular. The required angle between the tangents is 90 o . ∴ α = 90 ∘ ∴ sin θ + cos ( 90 ∘ + θ ) = sin θ + ( - sin θ ) = 0 Hence, sin θ + cos ( 90 ∘ + θ ) = 0
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

The equation of the given ellipse is x29+y216=1
The equation of the director circle is
x2 + y2 = 9 + 16
⇒ x2 + y2 = 25
On substituting the given point in the above equation, we get
9 + 16 = 25
∴ LHS = RHS
The given point lies on the director circle of the given ellipse.
The angle between the tangents drawn from the given point to the ellipse will be 90o .
∴ α=90∘
∴sinθ +cos(90∘+θ)
=sinθ+( - sinθ)
=0
Hence, sinθ + cos(90∘+θ)=0
Alternatively
Joint equation of PR and QR using the point (3, 4) and the ellipse x29+y216=1 is
SS1 = T2 (also called as S12=S×S11)
⇒ 96+1616-1x29+y216-1=3x9+4y16-12
⇒ x29+y216-1=x3+y4-12
⇒ x29+y216-1=x29+y216+xy6-y2-2x3
⇒ xy6-y2-2x3+1=0
∴ Coefficient of x2 + Coefficient of y2 = 0
The lines PQ and PR are perpendicular.
The required angle between the tangents is 90o.
∴ α=90∘
∴sinθ +cos(90∘+θ)
=sinθ+( - sinθ)
=0
Hence, sinθ + cos(90∘+θ)=0

questions.solutionText: Solution 12600 = 2 3 × 3 2 × 5 2 × 7 Number of divisors which are multiple of 3 = ( 3 + 1 ) ⋅ 2 ⋅ ( 2 + 1 ) ( 1 + 1 ) = 4 × 2 × 3 × 2 ∴ n 1 = 48 Number of divisors which are multiple of 14 = 3 × ( 2 + 1 ) × ( 2 + 1 ) × 1 = 3 × 3 × 3 ∴ n 2 = 27 Total = n 1 + n 2 = 48 + 27 = 75 .

questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
12600=23×32×52×7
Number of divisors which are multiple of 3
=(3+1)⋅2⋅(2+1)(1+1)=4×2×3×2∴n1=48

Number of divisors which are multiple of 14
=3×(2+1)×(2+1)×1=3×3×3∴n2=27 Total =n1+n2=48+27=75.

questions.solutionText: Solution Let h x = x , then g x = f x = h f x Since, composition of two continuous functions is continuous, is g continuous if f is continuous. Now, f x is an onto function. Since, co-domain of x is R and range of x is R . But g x is into function. Since, range of g x is 0 , ∞ but co-domain is given R . Let f x = x = > g x = x Now, f x is one-one function but g x is many-one function. Let f x = x = > g - x = x . Now, f x is differentiable for all x ∈ R but g x = x is not differentiable at x = 0 .

Since, co-domain of x is R and range of x is R . But g x is into function. Since, range of g x is 0 , ∞ but co-domain is given R . Let f x = x = > g x = x Now, f x is one-one function but g x is many-one function. Let f x = x = > g - x = x . Now, f x is differentiable for all x ∈ R but g x = x is not differentiable at x = 0 .
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Let hx=x, then
gx=fx=h fx
Since, composition of two continuous functions is continuous, is g continuous if f is continuous.
Now, fx is an onto function. Since, co-domain of x is R and range of x is R. But gx is into function. Since, range of gx is 0, ∞ but co-domain is given R.
Let fx=x=>gx=x Now, fx is one-one function but gx is many-one function.
Let fx=x=>g-x=x. Now, fx is differentiable for all x∈R but gx=x is not differentiable at x=0.

questions.text: Let n be the smallest positive integer such that 1 + 1 2 + 1 3 + … … + 1 n ≥ 4 Which one of the following statements is true?
questions.questionText: Let n be the smallest positive integer such that 1 + 1 2 + 1 3 + … … + 1 n ≥ 4 Which one of the following statements is true?
questions.question: Let n be the smallest positive integer such that 1 + 1 2 + 1 3 + … … + 1 n ≥ 4 Which one of the following statements is true?
questions.prompt: Let n be the smallest positive integer such that 1 + 1 2 + 1 3 + … … + 1 n ≥ 4 Which one of the following statements is true?
questions.html:
Let n be the smallest positive integer such that 1+12+13+……+1n≥4
Which one of the following statements is true?
questions.questionHtml:
Let n be the smallest positive integer such that 1+12+13+……+1n≥4
Which one of the following statements is true?
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
1+x<1+x+x22!+x33!+………1+x<ex
ln⁡(1+x)<x
x=1y
ln⁡(1+1y)<1y
ln⁡(y+1)−ln⁡y<1y
put y=1
ln⁡(2)−ln⁡(1)<11
put =y=2
ln⁡3−ln⁡2<12
put =y=3
ln⁡4−ln⁡3<13
put y=n
ln⁡(n+1)−ln⁡n<14
ln⁡(n+1)−ln⁡(1)<1+12+13+…1n
ln⁡(n+1)≤4
n≤e4−1
n≤60

options.label: B
options.html: nn+12,nn+12n+16
options.optionHtml: nn+12,nn+12n+16

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: If a variable takes the values as 0, 1, 2,....n with frequencies as n C 0 , n C 1 , n C 2 , ....., n C n respectively, then mean and variance are given as :
questions.questionText: If a variable takes the values as 0, 1, 2,....n with frequencies as n C 0 , n C 1 , n C 2 , ....., n C n respectively, then mean and variance are given as :
questions.question: If a variable takes the values as 0, 1, 2,....n with frequencies as n C 0 , n C 1 , n C 2 , ....., n C n respectively, then mean and variance are given as :
questions.prompt: If a variable takes the values as 0, 1, 2,....n with frequencies as n C 0 , n C 1 , n C 2 , ....., n C n respectively, then mean and variance are given as :
questions.html: If a variable takes the values as 0, 1, 2,....n with frequencies as nC0, nC1, nC2, ....., nCn respectively, then mean and variance are given as :
questions.questionHtml: If a variable takes the values as 0, 1, 2,....n with frequencies as nC0, nC1, nC2, ....., nCn respectively, then mean and variance are given as :
questions.solutionText: Solution Values: x = 0 , 1 , 2 , … , n with frequencies f x = n C x Total frequency: ∑ f x = ∑ n C x = 2 n Mean = ∑ x ⋅ n C x 2 n ∑ x = 0 n x ⋅ n C x = n ⋅ 2 n − 1 Mean = n ⋅ 2 n − 1 2 n = n 2 Variance = ∑ x 2 ⋅ n C x 2 n − ( n 2 ) 2 ∑ x = 0 n x 2 ⋅ n C x = n ( n + 1 ) ⋅ 2 n − 2 − n ⋅ 2 n − 2 + n ⋅ 2 n − 1 Using ∑ x 2 ⋅ n C x = n ( n − 1 ) ⋅ 2 n − 2 + n ⋅ 2 n − 1 ∑ x 2 ⋅ n C x 2 n = n ( n − 1 ) ⋅ 2 n − 2 + n ⋅ 2 n − 1 2 n = n ( n − 1 ) 4 + n 2 Variance = n ( n − 1 ) 4 + n 2 − n 2 4 = n 2 − n + 2 n − n 2 4 = n 4 Mean = n 2 , Variance = n 4 Answer: (3) n 2 , n 4
questions.solution: Solution Values: x = 0 , 1 , 2 , … , n with frequencies f x = n C x Total frequency: ∑ f x = ∑ n C x = 2 n Mean = ∑ x ⋅ n C x 2 n ∑ x = 0 n x ⋅ n C x = n ⋅ 2 n − 1 Mean = n ⋅ 2 n − 1 2 n = n 2 Variance = ∑ x 2 ⋅ n C x 2 n − ( n 2 ) 2 ∑ x = 0 n x 2 ⋅ n C x = n ( n + 1 ) ⋅ 2 n − 2 − n ⋅ 2 n − 2 + n ⋅ 2 n − 1 Using ∑ x 2 ⋅ n C x = n ( n − 1 ) ⋅ 2 n − 2 + n ⋅ 2 n − 1 ∑ x 2 ⋅ n C x 2 n = n ( n − 1 ) ⋅ 2 n − 2 + n ⋅ 2 n − 1 2 n = n ( n − 1 ) 4 + n 2 Variance = n ( n − 1 ) 4 + n 2 − n 2 4 = n 2 − n + 2 n − n 2 4 = n 4 Mean = n 2 , Variance = n 4 Answer: (3) n 2 , n 4
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Values: x=0,1,2,…,n with frequencies fx=nCx

Total frequency: ∑fx=∑nCx=2n

Mean =∑x⋅nCx2n

∑x=0nx⋅nCx=n⋅2n−1

Mean =n⋅2n−12n=n2

Variance =∑x2⋅nCx2n−(n2)2

∑x=0nx2⋅nCx=n(n+1)⋅2n−2−n⋅2n−2+n⋅2n−1

Using ∑x2⋅nCx=n(n−1)⋅2n−2+n⋅2n−1

∑x2⋅nCx2n=n(n−1)⋅2n−2+n⋅2n−12n=n(n−1)4+n2

Variance =n(n−1)4+n2−n24=n2−n+2n−n24=n4

Mean =n2, Variance =n4

Answer: (3) n2,n4

questions.text: Let I = ∫ x cos x + x sin x 2 d x = ( sin x - x cos x ) m . ( x sin x + cos x ) n + c . (where c is constant of integration) then the value of 3 m - 2 n is:
questions.questionText: Let I = ∫ x cos x + x sin x 2 d x = ( sin x - x cos x ) m . ( x sin x + cos x ) n + c . (where c is constant of integration) then the value of 3 m - 2 n is:
questions.question: Let I = ∫ x cos x + x sin x 2 d x = ( sin x - x cos x ) m . ( x sin x + cos x ) n + c . (where c is constant of integration) then the value of 3 m - 2 n is:
questions.prompt: Let I = ∫ x cos x + x sin x 2 d x = ( sin x - x cos x ) m . ( x sin x + cos x ) n + c . (where c is constant of integration) then the value of 3 m - 2 n is:
questions.html: Let I=∫xcosx+xsinx2dx=(sinx-xcosx)m.(xsinx+cosx)n+c. (where c is constant of integration) then the value of 3 m-2 n is:
questions.questionHtml: Let I=∫xcosx+xsinx2dx=(sinx-xcosx)m.(xsinx+cosx)n+c. (where c is constant of integration) then the value of 3 m-2 n is:
questions.solutionText: Solution I = ∫ x cos x ( x sin x + cos x ) 2 · x cos x d x Integrating by parts, I = - 1 x sin x + cos x · x cos x + ∫ 1 x sin x + cos x · cos x - x ( - sin x ) cos 2 x d x = - 1 x sin x + cos x x cos x + ∫ sec 2 x d x = - 1 x sin x + cos x x cos x + tan x + c = - x + x sin 2 x + sin x cos x cos x ( x sin x + cos x ) + c = - x cos 2 x + sin x cos x cos x ( x sin x + cos x ) + c = sin x - x cos x x sin x + cos x + c ⇒ m = 1 , n = - 1 ⇒ 3 m - 2 n = 3 + 2 = 5
questions.solution: Solution I = ∫ x cos x ( x sin x + cos x ) 2 · x cos x d x Integrating by parts, I = - 1 x sin x + cos x · x cos x + ∫ 1 x sin x + cos x · cos x - x ( - sin x ) cos 2 x d x = - 1 x sin x + cos x x cos x + ∫ sec 2 x d x = - 1 x sin x + cos x x cos x + tan x + c = - x + x sin 2 x + sin x cos x cos x ( x sin x + cos x ) + c = - x cos 2 x + sin x cos x cos x ( x sin x + cos x ) + c = sin x - x cos x x sin x + cos x + c ⇒ m = 1 , n = - 1 ⇒ 3 m - 2 n = 3 + 2 = 5
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

I=∫xcosx(xsinx+cosx)2·xcosxdx

Integrating by parts, I=-1xsinx+cosx·xcosx

+∫1xsinx+cosx·cosx-x(-sinx)cos2xdx

=-1xsinx+cosxxcosx+∫sec2xdx

=-1xsinx+cosxxcosx+tanx+c

=-x+xsin2x+sinxcosxcosx(xsinx+cosx)+c

=-xcos2x+sinxcosxcosx(xsinx+cosx)+c

=sinx-xcosxxsinx+cosx+c

⇒m=1,n=-1

⇒3m-2n=3+2=5

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: The eccentricity of an ellipse with its centre at the origin is 1 2 . If one of the directrices is x = 4 , then the equation of the ellipse is
questions.questionText: The eccentricity of an ellipse with its centre at the origin is 1 2 . If one of the directrices is x = 4 , then the equation of the ellipse is
questions.question: The eccentricity of an ellipse with its centre at the origin is 1 2 . If one of the directrices is x = 4 , then the equation of the ellipse is
questions.prompt: The eccentricity of an ellipse with its centre at the origin is 1 2 . If one of the directrices is x = 4 , then the equation of the ellipse is
questions.html: The eccentricity of an ellipse with its centre at the origin is 12. If one of the directrices is x=4, then the equation of the ellipse is
questions.questionHtml: The eccentricity of an ellipse with its centre at the origin is 12. If one of the directrices is x=4, then the equation of the ellipse is
questions.solutionText: Solution Since, equation of directrix is x = 4 . Then, the major axis of an ellipse is along X -axis. ∴ a e = 4 ⇒ a = 4 × 1 2 ∵ e = 1 2 ⇒ a = 2 Now, b 2 = a 2 1 - e 2 ∴ b 2 = 4 1 - 1 4 = 4 × 3 4 ⇒ b 2 = 3 Hence, equation of the ellipse is ⇒ x 2 4 + y 2 3 = 1 ⇒ 3 x 2 + 4 y 2 = 12 .
questions.solution: Solution Since, equation of directrix is x = 4 . Then, the major axis of an ellipse is along X -axis. ∴ a e = 4 ⇒ a = 4 × 1 2 ∵ e = 1 2 ⇒ a = 2 Now, b 2 = a 2 1 - e 2 ∴ b 2 = 4 1 - 1 4 = 4 × 3 4 ⇒ b 2 = 3 Hence, equation of the ellipse is ⇒ x 2 4 + y 2 3 = 1 ⇒ 3 x 2 + 4 y 2 = 12 .
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

Since, equation of directrix is x=4.

Then, the major axis of an ellipse is along X-axis.

∴ ae=4⇒a=4×12 ∵ e=12

⇒a=2

Now, b2=a21-e2

∴ b2=41-14=4×34

⇒ b2=3

Hence, equation of the ellipse is

⇒x24+y23=1

⇒3x2+4y2=12.

questions.question: If 2 p is the length of perpendicular from the origin to the lines x a + y b = 1 , then a 2 , 8 p 2 , b 2 are in
questions.prompt: If 2 p is the length of perpendicular from the origin to the lines x a + y b = 1 , then a 2 , 8 p 2 , b 2 are in
questions.html: If 2p is the length of perpendicular from the origin to the lines xa+yb=1, then a2,8p2,b2 are in
questions.questionHtml: If 2p is the length of perpendicular from the origin to the lines xa+yb=1, then a2,8p2,b2 are in
questions.solutionText: Solution We have 2 p = | 0 + 0 − 1 1 a 2 + 1 b 2 | ⇒ 1 a 2 + 1 b 2 = 1 4 p 2 ⇒ 1 a 2 + 1 b 2 = 2 8 p 2 ⇒ 1 a 2 , 1 8 p 2 , 1 b 2 are in A.P. ⇒ a 2 , 8 p 2 , p 2 are in H.P.
questions.solution: Solution We have 2 p = | 0 + 0 − 1 1 a 2 + 1 b 2 | ⇒ 1 a 2 + 1 b 2 = 1 4 p 2 ⇒ 1 a 2 + 1 b 2 = 2 8 p 2 ⇒ 1 a 2 , 1 8 p 2 , 1 b 2 are in A.P. ⇒ a 2 , 8 p 2 , p 2 are in H.P.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
We have 2p=|0+0−11a2+1b2|⇒1a2+1b2=14p2
⇒1a2+1b2=28p2⇒1a2,18p2,1b2 are in A.P.
⇒a2,8p2,p2 are in H.P.
